title: Tabernaemontana tomentosa
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana tomentosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Tabernaemontana and is native to tropical regions of Asia. The classification of Tabernaemontana tomentosa is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Magnoliophyta, class - Magnoliopsida, order - Gentianales, family - Apocynaceae, genus - Tabernaemontana, species - T. tomentosa. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies. The species is closely related to other members of the Tabernaemontana genus, which are known for their medicinal and ornamental value.
uses: Tabernaemontana tomentosa is traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help to treat wounds, skin infections, and fungal diseases. In some cultures, it is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots are also believed to have anthelmintic properties, which may help to expel intestinal parasites. However, the efficacy and safety of these uses have not been extensively scientifically studied, and more research is needed to confirm its medicinal value.
benefits: Tabernaemontana tomentosa, also known as the velvet tree or velvet poison tree, has been studied for its potential health benefits. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Tabernaemontana tomentosa has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Tabernaemontana tomentosa and to confirm its effectiveness for various medical conditions.

It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin infections. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the extent of its therapeutic potential and to develop safe and effective treatments.
constituents: Tabernaemontana tomentosa contains a variety of active constituents, including alkaloids such as voacamine and voacangine. These compounds are known for their potential biological activities, including antimalarial and anti-inflammatory effects. Other active constituents may include flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are also found in various medicinal plants. The exact composition of the plant's active constituents can vary depending on factors such as the plant's habitat and growing conditions. side_effects: Tabernaemontana tomentosa, also known as the hairy inside vine or the hairy inside, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. However, as with any plant-based remedy, there are potential side effects associated with its use. intro: Tabernaemontana tomentosa, also known as the "Chandana" or "Hairy Tabernaemontana", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are believed to aid in treating conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana tomentosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, including voacamine, voacristine, and tabernaemontanine. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused in herbal teas, while its roots are also used to make a powder or tablet for oral consumption.
